title: Explain the lifecycle of a Spring Bean and the role of post-processors.

answers.junior: Spring creates beans, sets their dependencies, and cleans them up when done. Post-processors can change beans after creation.
answers.middle: Bean lifecycle: instantiation, dependency injection, initialization, destruction. Post-processors modify beans before/after initialization, enabling features like AOP and custom logic.
answers.senior: BeanPostProcessor allows modification of beans before/after initialization. Used for proxy creation, custom logic, and infrastructure features. Multiple post-processors may interact; order matters.
answers.tricky: Bean lifecycle callbacks include InitializingBean, DisposableBean, @PostConstruct, @PreDestroy. Post-processors are key for AOP, security, and advanced customization. Improper use can cause subtle bugs or proxy issues.
